{{Infobox Royalty and Nobility | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."

The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>

[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}

Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>

In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. {{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}

=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>

== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002年, pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}
