Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004年, pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>

In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==

[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. {{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}

The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
[[Category:Tombs in Japan]] [[Category:Sanage Shrine]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Toyota, Aichi]] [[ja:大碓命墓]]
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子|12=draft}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|12=draft}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取|12=draft}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|12=draft}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|12=draft}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|12=draft}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|12=draft}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王|12=draft}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造|12=draft}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|12=draft}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|12=draft}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|12=draft}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}

The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|12=draft}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|12=draft}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王|12=draft}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造|12=draft}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|12=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|12=draft}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|12=draft}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|12=draft}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|12=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|12=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | Rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>

However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.comment: 45 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大碓皇子 up to rev 97365198
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 大碓皇子 | 各国語表記 = おおうすのみこ | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = [[景行天皇]]皇子 | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = <!-- 日本の皇族のみ --> | 出生日 = 景行天皇12年? | 生地 = | 死亡日 = 景行天皇52年? | 没地 = [[猿投山]]? | 埋葬日 = | 埋葬地 = 大碓命墓（[[愛知県]][[豊田市]]猿投町鷲取、[[猿投神社]]内） | 配偶者1 = 兄遠子、弟遠子 | 配偶者2 = （兄比売、弟比売） | 子女 = 押黒之兄日子王、押黒弟日子王 | 父親 = [[景行天皇]] | 母親 = [[播磨稲日大郎姫]] | 役職 = | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''大碓皇子'''（おおうすのみこ{{Sfn|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}/おほうすのみこ、景行天皇12年? - 景行天皇52年?）は、[[記紀]]等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[皇族]]（王族）。
『[[日本書紀]]』では「大碓皇子」または「'''大碓命'''（おおうすのみこと）」、『[[古事記]]』では「大碓命」と表記される。
第12代[[景行天皇]][[皇子]]で、[[ヤマトタケル]]（日本武尊/倭建命、小碓命）とは双子とされるが、ヤマトタケル誕生以前の景行天皇4年に美濃に遣わされた記録があり矛盾する。
== 系譜 == 『[[日本書紀]]』によれば、第12代[[景行天皇]]と、[[皇后]]の[[播磨稲日大郎姫]]（はりまのいなびのおおいらつめ、稲日稚郎姫）との間に生まれた[[皇子]]である。同書では、同母兄弟として[[ヤマトタケル|小碓尊]]（おうす<をうす>のみこと、日本童男/日本武尊）のみの名を挙げ、大碓命とは双子とする<ref name="日本書紀">『新編日本古典文学全集 2 日本書紀 (1)』小学館、2002年（ジャパンナレッジ版）、pp. 341-343, 347, 369-371。</ref>。
『[[古事記]]』においても、景行天皇と、[[稚武彦命|若建吉備津日子]]（吉備臣らの祖）の娘の針間之伊那毘能大郎女との間に生まれた皇子とする。ただし同書では、同母兄として櫛角別王、同母弟として小碓命（倭男具那命/倭建命、双子の記載はない）・倭根子命・[[神櫛皇子|神櫛王]]の名を挙げる<ref name="古事記">『新編日本古典文学全集 1 古事記』小学館、2004年（ジャパンナレッジ版）、pp. 213, 215-217。</ref>。
妻子について『古事記』では、[[大根王]]（おおねのみこ、[[本巣国造]]・[[三野前国造]]の祖）の娘の兄比売（えひめ）・弟比売（おとひめ）を娶り、兄比売とは押黒之兄日子王を、弟比売とは押黒弟日子王を儲けたとする<ref name="古事記"/>。
== 記録 == === 日本書紀 === 『日本書紀』によれば、大碓命・小碓尊ら双子出生の際、天皇が怪しんで臼に向かって叫んだので、「碓（うす）」の名がついたという<ref name="日本書紀"/>。
次いで同書景行天皇4年2月是月条によれば、天皇が[[神骨]]（かむほね、[[三野前国造|美濃国造]]）の娘の兄遠子（えとおこ）・弟遠子（おととおこ）姉妹が美人であると聞き、大碓命がその視察に遣わされたが、大碓命は密通して復命せず、天皇から恨まれたという<ref name="日本書紀"/>{{Sfn|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}。この月には天皇自身が美濃に行幸しているのに大碓皇子を美濃に派遣するのは不合理なため、下記の40年7月16日条の記事の一部が誤って紛れ込んだとも考えられる。その場合は生年の矛盾も解消する。
また同書景行天皇40年7月16日条によれば、天皇が[[蝦夷]]平定の適任を群臣に問うと、西征を終えた日本武尊は大碓皇子に任せることを進上した。しかし大碓皇子は草むらに逃げ隠れたため、使者をして召還させられ、天皇から責められたのち、[[美濃国]]に封じられた。そして[[牟義都国造|身毛津君]]・守君の祖になったという<ref name="日本書紀"/>（結局東征は日本武尊が行なった）{{Sfn|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
=== 古事記 === 『古事記』によれば、天皇が大根王（三野国造の祖）の娘の兄比売・弟比売姉妹が美人であると聞き、大碓命がその視察に遣わされた。しかし大碓命は密通し、天皇には偽って別の女性を献じた。そして大碓命は、兄比売とは押黒之兄日子王を、弟比売とは押黒弟日子王を儲け、押黒之兄日子王は三野之宇泥須和気の祖に、押黒弟日子王は牟宜都君（[[牟義都国造]]）らの祖になったという<ref name="古事記"/>{{Sfn|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
またある時、大碓命が朝夕の食膳に出てこないので、天皇が小碓命に「ねぎし教えさとせ（ねんごろに教えよ）」と命じた。しかし5日経っても大碓命が出てこなかったので、小碓命にどう教えたのか問うと、大碓命が明け方に厠に入った時、捕まえてつかみつぶし、手足をもぎとって薦に包んで投げ捨てた、と言ったという（「ねぎし」の誤解による）<ref name="古事記"/>。
== 墓 == [[陵墓|墓]]は、[[宮内庁]]により[[愛知県]][[豊田市]]猿投町鷲取の[[猿投神社]]内にある'''大碓命墓'''（おおうすのみことのはか、{{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}）に治定されている<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999年、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref>。宮内庁上の形式は[[円墳|円丘]]。
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[猿投神社]]（[[愛知県]][[豊田市]]）}}]] 上記の墓は[[猿投山]]の山中、猿投神社の西宮後方に所在する。この猿投神社では、大碓命は主祭神に祀られている。同社社伝によれば、大碓命は美濃に封じられてのち当地方の開拓に尽くしたが、景行天皇52年に猿投山に登る中途で蛇毒のため42歳で亡くなったという。
一方、歴史学者の荊木美行も、大碓命もしくはそのモデルとなった王族がヤマト王権の美濃平定において重要な役割を果たしてそのままこの地を支配したとする見方を取るが、猿投神社説は記紀にも[[延喜式]]諸陵式にも記載されていないことから比較的後世に作られた説であるとした上で、大碓命の事績からして美濃国内に陵墓があったと考えるのが適切であると考えて規模と築造年代から[[岐阜県]]大垣市にある[[昼飯大塚古墳]]がその有力な候補であるとしている<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>。
== 後裔氏族 == 大碓皇子について、『日本書紀』では[[牟義都国造|身毛津君]]・守君の祖とし、『古事記』では守君・大田君・嶋田君・三野之宇泥須和気・牟宜都君らの祖とする{{Sfn|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 牟義公 - 景行天皇皇子の大碓命の後。 * 左京皇別 守公 - 牟義公同氏。大碓命の後。 * 河内国皇別 大田宿禰 - 大碓命の後。 * 河内国皇別 守公 - 牟義公同祖。大碓命の後。 * 河内国皇別 阿礼首 - 守公同祖。大碓命の後。 * 和泉国皇別 池田首 - 景行天皇皇子の大碓命の後。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大碓皇子|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=978-4642014588|ref={{Harvid|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}}}
== 関連項目 == * [[ヤマトタケル]]
{{デフォルトソート:おおうすのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本出身の双子]] [[Category:景行天皇の子女]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおうすのみこ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[猿投神社]]（[[愛知県]][[豊田市]]）}}]] 上記の墓は[[猿投山]]の山中、猿投神社の西宮後方に所在する。この猿投神社では、大碓命は主祭神に祀られている。同社社伝によれば、大碓命は美濃に封じられてのち当地方の開拓に尽くしたが、景行天皇52年に猿投山に登る中途で蛇毒のため42歳で亡くなったという。
一方、歴史学者の荊木美行も、大碓命もしくはそのモデルとなった王族がヤマト王権の美濃平定において重要な役割を果たしてそのままこの地を支配したとする見方を取るが、猿投神社説は記紀にも[[延喜式]]諸陵式にも記載されていないことから比較的後世に作られた説であるとした上で、大碓命の事績からして美濃国内に陵墓があったと考えるのが適切であると考えて規模と築造年代から[[岐阜県]]大垣市にある[[昼飯大塚古墳]]がその有力な候補であるとしている<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022年、234-252・257頁。</ref>。
== 後裔氏族 == 大碓皇子について、『日本書紀』では[[牟義都国造|身毛津君]]・守君の祖とし、『古事記』では守君・大田君・嶋田君・三野之宇泥須和気・牟宜都君らの祖とする{{Sfn|大碓皇子（古代氏族）|2010年}}。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では、次の氏族が後裔として記載されている。 * 左京皇別 牟義公 - 景行天皇皇子の大碓命の後。 * 左京皇別 守公 - 牟義公同氏。大碓命の後。 * 河内国皇別 大田宿禰 - 大碓命の後。 * 河内国皇別 守公 - 牟義公同祖。大碓命の後。 * 河内国皇別 阿礼首 - 守公同祖。大碓命の後。 * 和泉国皇別 池田首 - 景行天皇皇子の大碓命の後。

{{デフォルトソート:おおうすのみこ}} [[Category:古墳時代以前の男性皇族]] [[Category:日本出身の双子]] [[Category:景行天皇の子女]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおうすのみこ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | Rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|qq=draft|qq=draft}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = {{ill|Ōusu no Miko|ja|大碓皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōusu no Miko|qq=}} | native_name = おおうすのみこ | house = | image = | caption = | relation = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}'s prince | titles = | full_name = | Rank = | honorific_prefix = | birth_date = Emperor Keikō's 12th year? | birth_place = | death_date = Emperor Keikō's 52nd year? | death_place = {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Sanage|qq=}}? | burial_date = | burial_place = Ōusu no Mikoto's Tomb ({{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市猿投町鷲取|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota, Aichi|qq=}}) | spouse1 = Ani-toko and Ototo-toko | spouse2 = (Ani-hime and Ototo-hime) | children = Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko | father = {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} | mother = {{ill|Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}} | position = | religion = | signature = }}
'''Ōusu no Miko''' (大碓皇子, also おおうすのみこ) was a prince of ancient {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} and a member of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|ja|皇室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial House of Japan|qq=}}. He is described in various ancient chronicles, including the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, where his name appears as either "Ōusu no Miko" or "Ōusu no Mikoto."
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}, the 12th emperor of Japan, and twin brother to {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. However, some accounts conflict, mentioning Ōusu's activities in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}} before Yamato Takeru’s birth.
== Genealogy == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Ōusu was born to Emperor Keikō and {{ill|Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|ja|播磨稲日大郎姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Inabi no Ōiratsume|qq=}}, the empress. The same text identifies his twin brother as {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}, also called Ousu no Mikoto.<ref name="NihonShoki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|qq=}}』2: 日本書紀 (1), 小学館, 2002nd year , pp. 341–371.</ref>
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} describes him similarly but lists additional siblings, including {{ill|Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|ja|櫛角別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushitsuno Wakaya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} (as a younger brother, not a twin).<ref name="Kojiki">『{{ill|Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|ja|新編日本古典文学全集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|qq=}}』1: 古事記, 小学館, 2004th year , pp. 213–217.</ref>
His wives were Ani-hime and Ototo-hime, daughters of the {{ill|Ōne no Mikoto|ja|大根王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōne no Mikoto|qq=}}, an ancestor of the rulers of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|三野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}. Ōusu fathered two sons: Oshikuro no Ani-Hiko and Oshikuro no Ototo-Hiko.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Historical Records == === Nihon Shoki === The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} narrates that upon the twins’ birth, Emperor Keikō named them after uttering the word "Usu" (臼, "mortar") while holding suspicion towards them.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
Later, in the emperor's 4th year, Ōusu was sent to investigate rumors about two beautiful sisters, Ani-toko and Ototo-toko, in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}. However, he engaged in secret relations with them and failed to report back, earning his father's anger.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
In the 40th year, Emperor Keikō tasked Ōusu with leading a military campaign against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, but Ōusu fled and hid. Following this, he was assigned to Mino, where he became the ancestor of several regional clans.<ref name="NihonShoki"/>
=== Kojiki === The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} similarly records that Ōusu was dispatched to Mino Province but engaged in secret relations with Ani-hime and Ototo-hime. Through these unions, he fathered descendants who became the forebears of various clans.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
One notable anecdote recounts Emperor Keikō ordering Yamato Takeru to reprimand Ōusu for missing imperial meals. Mistaking the order, Yamato Takeru reportedly killed and dismembered Ōusu.<ref name="Kojiki"/>
== Tomb ==
The [[Tomb (Japan)|tomb]] is officially designated by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as the '''Ōusuno Mikoto no Haka''' (大碓命墓, {{Coord|35|11|56.42|N|137|9|54.32|E|region:JP-23|name=大碓命墓}}), located within the {{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota, Aichi|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture.<ref>『宮内庁書陵部陵墓地形図集成』 学生社、1999th year 、巻末の「歴代順陵墓等一覧」表。</ref><ref>宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934th year 、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）9コマ。</ref> The official designation by the Imperial Household Agency categorizes it as a [[Round Kofun|circular mound]] (円丘).
[[File:猿投神社03.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Toyota, Aichi|ja|豊田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota, Aichi|qq=}})}]]] The above-mentioned tomb is located within the mountains of {{ill|Mount Sanage|ja|猿投山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Sanage|qq=}}, behind the western shrine of Sanage Shrine. At this shrine, Ōusuno Mikoto is venerated as the main deity. According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keinoki Miyuki|qq=}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki, Gifu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamoru no Kimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Sukune|qq=}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Saki|qq=}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arei no Kubi|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province|qq=}})
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}

According to the shrine's tradition, Ōusuno Mikoto was assigned to {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}} and contributed to developing the local region. However, he died at the age of 42 during an ascent of Mount Sanage due to snake venom in the 52nd year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}’s reign.
On the other hand, historian {{ill|Keinoki Miyuki|ja|荊木美行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keinoki Miyuki|qq=}} suggests that Ōusuno Mikoto, or a royal figure modeled after him, played an essential role in the Yamato court’s conquest of Mino Province and subsequently governed the area. However, Keinoki considers the Sanage Shrine theory to have emerged relatively later, as it is not mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, or the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}. Based on Ōusuno Mikoto’s achievements, Keinoki argues that his tomb would more plausibly be located within Mino Province. Given the scale and construction period, Keinoki identifies the {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|ja|昼飯大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōgaki, Gifu|ja|大垣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki, Gifu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture, as a strong candidate.<ref>荊木美行「大碓命伝説の虚と実」『日本書紀の成立と史料性』燃焼社、2022nd year 、234-252・257頁。</ref>
== Descendant Clans == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} identify Ōusu as the ancestor of various regional clans, including: * {{ill|Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|牟義都国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and {{ill|Mamoru no Kimi|ja|守君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamoru no Kimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}) * {{ill|Ōta no Sukune|ja|大田宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta no Sukune|qq=}} and {{ill|Ikeda no Saki|ja|池田首|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda no Saki|qq=}} ({{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}) * {{ill|Arei no Kubi|ja|阿礼首|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arei no Kubi|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumi Province|ja|和泉国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Province|qq=}})
